summary: Each PT6A-27 engine has its own independent pressure/scavenge/breather oil system. The oil tank (2.3 USG total, 1.5 USG usable) is integral with the compressor inlet case. Engine oil lubricates all bearings and gearboxes, acts as a heat-exchange medium in the fuel heater, provides oil pressure for the torquemeter and propeller governor (CSU), and drives the propeller feathering pump. Maximum normal consumption is 0.2 lb/hr. Oil level must be checked within 10 minutes of shutdown; if more than 10 minutes have elapsed the engine must be dry-motored first to drain oil from upper galleries before a valid reading can be taken.

references.locator: Section 4 Engine / Section 13 Power Plant Limitations

components.name: Oil Tank
components.description: Integral with the compressor inlet case at the rear of the engine. Total capacity 2.3 USG (1.9 Imp gal); usable 1.5 USG. Filler cap accessed through the nacelle top door, approximately 9.5 ft above the ground. Oil specification: synthetic, conforming to CPWA 202. For a current list of approved oils see P&WC Service Bulletin 1001.

components.name: Oil Pressure Pump
components.description: Gear-type pump driven by the accessory gearbox (NG shaft). Pressurises oil from the tank and distributes it to all engine bearings, reduction and accessory gearboxes, and through the fuel heater. Also provides oil pressure to the torquemeter and the CSU governor pump.

components.name: Oil Scavenge System
components.description: Multiple scavenge pumps (also on the accessory gearbox) return oil from the bearing sumps and gearbox sumps to the oil tank. Scavenged oil passes through the fuel heater (oil-to-fuel heat exchanger) to cool before returning to the tank. When the engine is shut down, oil drains from the upper galleries; if the engine sits for more than 10 minutes, excess oil must be motored clear before checking the dipstick.

components.name: Fuel Heater (Fuel-Oil Heat Exchanger)
components.description: In-line heat exchanger in the engine low-pressure fuel supply line. Hot engine oil warms the fuel before it reaches the HP pump, preventing ice crystal formation at altitude. Also pre-conditions (warms) oil on cold starts, contributing to faster oil temperature rise. The fuel heater is why a minimum oil temperature of 55°C is recommended before take-off at maximum power.

components.name: Oil Pressure Gauge
components.description: Indicates engine oil supply pressure in psi. Normally 80–100 psi (PT6A-27) or 85–105 psi (PT6A-34) at gas generator speeds above 72% NG with oil temperature between 60–70°C. A single gauge per engine is located on the instrument panel. The gauge is critical for detecting oil system failures — below 40 psi is considered unsafe.

components.name: Oil Temperature Gauge
components.description: Indicates oil inlet temperature in °C. Normal range 0–99°C for take-off; recommended operating range 74–80°C for service life. A minimum of 10°C is required for take-off. The minimum oil temperature for fuel heater operation at take-off power is 55°C.

components.name: Chip Detector
components.description: Magnetic plug in the reduction gearbox oil drain path. If metallic particles (chips) from gear or bearing failure accumulate on the magnet, the CHIP DET caution light illuminates on the instrument panel. Illumination requires engineering inspection before further flight — it can indicate imminent gearbox failure.

controls.name: Oil Filler Cap
controls.description: Accessed through nacelle top door. Always ensure the cap is secured before flight. Gravity fill from container; no pressurised fill connection.

limits.note: 40–80 psi: undesirable — land with caution at reduced power. Below 40 psi: UNSAFE — engine shutdown or land immediately. 40–80 psi acceptable for ground ops only.

limits.parameter: Oil Temperature — Minimum for Fuel Heater at Take-Off Power
Oil Level Check Time Limit After Shutdown
limits.value: Within 10 minutes — motor engine first if >10 min elapsed
limits.note: After more than 10 minutes, oil drains from upper galleries and a false low reading results. Dry-motor (without fuel) for 30 seconds before checking.
